coupling_issues.description: Modules directly depending on specific implementations rather than abstractions

locations.coupling: Direct instantiation of PostfixLogParser with hard-coded container name
locations.impact: Cannot easily substitute different log parser or mock for testing
Tightly coupled to BullMQ implementation; switching queue systems would require changes

locations.coupling: Direct instantiation of child_process and direct MongoDB model usage
locations.impact: Parser is tightly coupled to Docker, specific container name, and DeliveryEvent model

locations.coupling: Direct instantiation of nodemailer transports with hard-coded provider logic
locations.code: this.transports = [ nodemailer.createTransport({ host, port, secure: port === 465, auth: { user, pass }, pool: true, maxConnections: 5, maxMessages: 100 }) ];
locations.impact: Tightly coupled to nodemailer and specific provider logic (Brevo/AWS SES)

locations.coupling: Direct use of jsonwebtoken and bcryptjs libraries
locations.impact: Tightly coupled to specific JWT and bcrypt implementations

coupling_issues.description: Modules coupled through shared global state or singletons

locations.coupling: Singleton pattern using hard-coded 'global' singleton field
locations.code: singleton: { type: String, default: 'global', unique: true }
locations.impact: All warmup functionality tightly coupled to single global instance

locations.code:
let parserInstance = null;
const getParser = () => { if (!parserInstance) { parserInstance = new PostfixLogParser(); } return parserInstance; };
locations.impact: Tight coupling to single parser instance makes testing difficult
Tight coupling to single relay pool instance
Tight coupling through global state for logout handling

coupling_issues.description: Inappropriate use of inheritance leading to tight coupling

locations.coupling: All models directly extend mongoose.Schema and inherit Mongoose-specific methods
locations.impact: Models are tightly coupled to Mongoose ODM; switching ORM would require complete rewrite

coupling_issues.description: Functions with long parameter lists indicating tight coupling

locations.coupling: _buildEvent method extracts many fields from regex match
locations.impact: Method is tightly coupled to the specific regex match structure

locations.coupling: launchCampaign function accesses many nested properties from request and campaign objects
locations.impact: Function is tightly coupled to the specific structure of campaign and domain objects

coupling_issues.description: Modules coupled through specific exception types or error handling

locations.coupling: Direct checking for specific error codes like 11000 (duplicate key)
locations.code: if (error.code === 11000) { return res.status(409).json({ message: 'Duplicate key error' }); }

locations.coupling: Checking for specific JWT error names
locations.impact: Coupled to specific jsonwebtoken error types

coupling_issues.description: Missing dependency injection patterns that would reduce coupling

locations.coupling: Direct require() of models and services
locations.code:
const Campaign = require('../models/Campaign');
const Domain = require('../models/Domain');
locations.impact: Controllers cannot easily use mocks or alternative implementations

locations.code:
const { Resolver } = require('dns/promises');
const crypto = require('crypto');
locations.impact: Utilities cannot easily use mocks or alternative implementations

locations.coupling: Hard-coded dependencies on specific libraries and models
locations.impact: Parser cannot be easily tested with mocks or used in different environments

summary: The codebase shows significant coupling issues, particularly tight coupling to specific implementations (Docker, BullMQ, nodemailer, Mongoose), use of singleton patterns creating global state coupling, and lack of dependency injection making testing and flexibility difficult. These issues increase the rigidity of the system and make it harder to change implementations or test components in isolation.
